title: Paranormal Parentage

jokes.text: Happy Halloween parties, everyone. You all look great... ham.
jokes.setup: Shirley trying to compliment everyone's costumes
jokes.punchline: Accidentally calling Jeff 'ham' instead of completing her thought
jokes.explanation: Classic Shirley malapropism - economical and character-specific, gets a solid laugh

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Oh Jeff, that's such a... ham costume!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Did you just call me ham?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I was going to say handsome! Ham-handsome? No, that's not... I meant you look very... ham.

jokes.text: Pig. Let me guess. You're a flimsy excuse to be shirtless, wearing silk underwear.
jokes.setup: Jeff correcting Shirley and setting up to mock someone's costume
jokes.punchline: Assumes it's a lazy costume designed for sex appeal
jokes.explanation: Sharp, character-specific wit from Jeff - cynical and well-constructed

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's not a 'sexy nurse,' Shirley. It's a nurse who is sexy. There's a difference.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: One is a profession, the other is a lazy costume designed for sex appeal.

jokes.text: And you are as wrong as you are welcome.
jokes.setup: Jeff responding to being called out about his costume
jokes.punchline: Parallel structure joke that sounds complimentary but isn't
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect parallel structure - economical and clever

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What? I look like a sexy fireman. You look like a sexy... construction worker. See? We're matching.

jokes.text: Annie, no. I meant the boxing match ring girl. They're the sexy ones that hold the round numbers.
jokes.setup: Annie misunderstood what kind of 'ring girl' Jeff meant
jokes.punchline: Jeff having to awkwardly explain the sexual nature of his costume idea
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy from the misunderstanding - character-driven awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No, no, no, Annie. A ring girl. You know, like at boxing matches? The attractive woman who walks around with the numbers?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Oh my god, I thought you meant... I was going to say yes!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yes to what?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Being your ring girl! I was already mentally preparing myself for the humiliation.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That IS what I meant.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: But you just said—

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I know what I said. I'm just clarifying that it's a legitimate costume. It's not... it's not a stripper thing.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: It's a little bit of a stripper thing.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It really isn't.

jokes.text: This is why we can't just text about it, Jeff. I don't watch sports.
jokes.setup: The miscommunication about the costume
jokes.punchline: Annie's innocent explanation reveals the communication gap
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that explains the misunderstanding naturally

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I was just trying to help with the costume! I thought you said you needed it by Friday, so I stayed up all night working on it.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, I said I needed it by next Friday. That's a week away.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, now you have options!

jokes.text: Pretty freaky-deaky, huh?
jokes.setup: Annie thinks she understands the sexy boxing ring girl costume
jokes.punchline: Her innocent attempt at being seductive is awkwardly off-brand
jokes.explanation: Annie's awkward attempt at being sultry fits her character perfectly but isn't groundbreaking comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I'm going to be a sexy boxing ring girl. I'll wear the outfit and carry the round number card, and I'll do this thing where I bite my lip and walk slowly around the ring.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: What? I'm being seductive. I researched it. I watched three whole minutes of a boxing match.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I'm going to smile really big and really make eye contact, because confidence is sexy, and I'll compliment everyone's boxing technique and ask them about their hydration levels.

jokes.text: You should probably warn your boyfriend's boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Discussion about couples costumes
jokes.punchline: Britta's confusing way of referring to Troy and Abed's relationship
jokes.explanation: Clever way to describe the Troy-Abed dynamic without being explicit

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You guys should do a couples costume!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: We're not a couple.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Oh, I know. That's why it would be funny. You two live together, you do everything together, you finish each other's sentences...

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We're just best friends.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Exactly. Like a couple.

jokes.text: We do some things. We do a lot of things. Not all the things. Things.
jokes.setup: Trying to defend her relationship with Troy
jokes.punchline: Verbal breakdown into just saying 'things' repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and breakdown - very Britta-specific verbal fumbling

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: We're just friends! There's nothing weird going on between me and Troy.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I mean, we hang out. We do things. We say things. We like things.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Things, things, things. Just... things.

jokes.text: She put a pencil through his face.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Pierce wasn't invited to the party
jokes.punchline: Casual delivery of violent imagery
jokes.explanation: Abed's deadpan delivery of shocking information - economical and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Pierce wasn't invited because he's dead. I killed him. I didn't want to, but he kept doing that thing where he leans back in his chair and I just snapped. I wrapped the phone cord around his neck and dragged him into the study. Then I pushed him down the stairs. His body's still there.

jokes.text: You're not watching TV. - No, I'll be watching Annie. She started watching Cougar town re-runs, which makes Annie my third favorite show
jokes.setup: Annie pointing out it's a party, not TV time
jokes.punchline: Abed ranking Annie as a TV show in his personal hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Abed's meta perspective and obsessive categorization - highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: It's a party, Abed. You should be socializing, not watching TV.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Annie, you ARE a TV show. You're somewhere between a procedural drama and a very special episode.

jokes.text: Ooh, that's right. Better not miss the Merlot. Clink! What? You fall into it.
jokes.setup: Reference to the Cougar Town Halloween special
jokes.punchline: Troy's confused attempt to participate in wine culture
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent misunderstanding of wine culture - endearing character moment

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, we're doing Cougar Town? I love that show! I watched the Halloween special like three times.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: So... do I need to bring wine? I don't know anything about wine. Is it supposed to be cold? Hot? Do you drink it out of a shoe?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'll just bring some Sunny D and call it 'Sun Wine.' That's wine, right? It's got 'wine' in the name!

jokes.text: got killed by Jeff.
jokes.setup: Explaining Pierce's situation after his father's death
jokes.punchline: Casual blame assignment to Jeff for the death
jokes.explanation: Casual delivery of dramatic accusation - good callback humor

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Pierce's father passed away, and honestly, this is all Jeff's fault.

jokes.text: Heads, lock up your brains. Britta's on the prowl for fresh therapy meat.
jokes.setup: Britta showing interest in Pierce's psychological state
jokes.punchline: Jeff's warning framed as a zombie movie announcement
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor mixing therapy with horror movie tropes - very Jeff

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey. I'm gonna stop you right there. You do not want to go down this road. Trust me. Once you start trying to psychoanalyze Pierce, there is no coming back. It's like a zombie movie, except the zombie is also your friend, and instead of eating your brain, he's just gonna talk about his various ailments and his ex-wives until your brain eats itself.

jokes.text: He is a geriatric toddler, throwing the most elaborate tantrums money can buy
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining Pierce's behavior pattern
jokes.punchline: Oxymoronic description that perfectly captures Pierce's immaturity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character description - economical, accurate, and highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce is like a sophisticated man-child.

jokes.text: Ding, ding, ding! To your corners, fighters. Someone save me a towel.
jokes.setup: Tension building between Jeff and Annie
jokes.punchline: Troy treating it like a boxing match, complete with ring girl reference
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent callback to the boxing theme - solid but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Hold up! We got a situation here.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Jeff, you're in your corner. Annie, you're in your corner.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Okay, round one... FIGHT!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: And ladies and gentlemen, we need a ring girl! Where's our ring girl?

jokes.text: Hello, Dr. Perry's Office. Damaged psyche? Yes, I'll accept the charges.
jokes.setup: Britta sees an opportunity to practice psychology
jokes.punchline: Her phone operator bit treating psychology like a collect call
jokes.explanation: Good Britta character moment showing her eagerness to psychoanalyze. The collect call metaphor is clever.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Thank you for calling Psychology Hotline. This call may be recorded for quality assurance. If you're having suicidal thoughts, press 1. If you're experiencing anxiety, press 2. If you just need someone to tell you that your problems aren't real and you're being dramatic, stay on the line.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: We're sorry, all our therapists are busy helping people with real problems. Please try your issues again later.

jokes.text: It's like David Lee Roth threw up Miami Vice.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Pierce's house decor
jokes.punchline: Specific 80s reference combining a person and aesthetic
jokes.explanation: Vivid, specific imagery mixing pop culture references - classic Jeff snark

jokes.text: Maybe Pierce isn't doing so well since he lost his father and then his inheritance in a video game battle with Gilbert, an illegitimate half-Brother he never knew he had. I guess I've never said it out loud.
jokes.setup: Annie trying to defend Pierce's emotional state
jokes.punchline: Realizes how insane Pierce's life sounds when summarized
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating recap that highlights how absurd Community's continuity is - meta without being forced

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce has been through a lot. He's dealing with a lot of emotional baggage.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: His father was distant, he's had multiple divorces, he lost his business...

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, when you say it all out loud like that, Pierce's life sounds insane.

jokes.text: June 30, 1989. Theatrical release of do the right thing.
jokes.setup: Troy remembering a specific date from the house tour
jokes.punchline: Abed immediately knowing what happened on that date
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - his encyclopedic film knowledge on display

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, remember that day during the house tour when we went into the basement?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: February 14th, 2010. You stepped on a nail.

jokes.text: Oh, put a sock in it, shorts.
jokes.setup: Someone (likely Annie) expressing concern about the situation
jokes.punchline: Pierce's dismissive response with clothing-based insult
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce dismissiveness - in character but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce, this is serious. We need to talk about what happened.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Annie, please. I've forgotten more about life than you'll ever know. Now if you'll excuse me, I need to get back to my nap. And stop wearing those cardigans—you look like a librarian's grandmother.

jokes.text: don't be. I've been locked in way worse places than this. Oh, not against my will.
jokes.setup: Comforting Pierce about being embarrassed
jokes.punchline: Troy's innocent overshare about consensual confinement
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent innuendo - sweet character moment with adult implications

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: It's okay Pierce, we all have things we're embarrassed about. I mean, I'm into some weird stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Like, I enjoy being tied up. But it's all consensual and safe, you know?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: The point is, whatever you're embarrassed about, it's probably not that weird.

jokes.text: This place has not been dusted in months, and he starts with the panic room? No sale. He's not a sad old man. He's a sad old liar.
jokes.setup: Jeff analyzing Pierce's story for logical inconsistencies
jokes.punchline: Concluding with parallel structure insult
jokes.explanation: Good detective work from Jeff with a solid punchline structure

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce, your story doesn't make sense. You said you were in Morocco, but then you mentioned the Eiffel Tower. That's in France.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, I traveled around.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You also said it happened in 1987, but you were in prison in 1987.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Different prison.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: And you claimed you saved the woman's life, but then said she died the next day.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I saved her life that day.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce, you're a liar, a fraud, and a man whose stories are about as coherent as your life choices—which is to say, not at all.

jokes.text: I had to come up with that awesome dusting story
jokes.setup: Pierce admitting he was lying
jokes.punchline: Describing his obviously bad lie as 'awesome'
jokes.explanation: Pierce's obliviousness to how transparent his lie was - good character work

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Okay, I was lying.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That was awesome.

jokes.text: aha! I mean, not, aha, your dad is dead, But, aha, I knew it. Ghost dad, exploring your haunted mansion for secret codes?
jokes.setup: Britta getting excited about Pierce's ghost story
jokes.punchline: Having to clarify she's not celebrating his father's death
jokes.explanation: Classic Britta foot-in-mouth moment - good cringe comedy

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Wait, Pierce's father died? That's amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No, no, I mean — for the ghost story! That's perfect for a ghost story!

jokes.text: What in the Scooby-Doo is happening to you people?
jokes.setup: Jeff's frustration with everyone believing in ghosts
jokes.punchline: Cartoon reference substituted as profanity
jokes.explanation: Clever reference that also acknowledges the genre they're in - good meta humor

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You guys, there are no ghosts! It's not real!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Stop being ridiculous. This is insane.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh, for the love of... Scooby-Doo!

jokes.text: I'm sure as stuff not your sexy little ring girl.
jokes.setup: Annie being called naive
jokes.punchline: Angrily rejecting the sexualized costume idea with mild profanity
jokes.explanation: Annie's innocent version of swearing while rejecting objectification - character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I'm not naive! And I'm not wearing some sexy nurse outfit or whatever. That's bullshit.

jokes.text: Can it, ham. She's also my ride.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to respond to Annie's anger
jokes.punchline: Annie cutting him off, Jeff admitting practical dependence
jokes.explanation: Good callback and Jeff's deflating admission - nice beat

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, I know you're upset, but if you just let me explain—

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: No, Jeff. I'm done listening to your excuses.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Look, you're right. You're always right. I can't even make my own coffee without you.

jokes.text: I didn't leave my short-term memory at Coachella.
jokes.setup: Troy giving unnecessary directions
jokes.punchline: Shirley's specific cultural reference for memory loss
jokes.explanation: Unexpected cultural reference from Shirley - slightly out of character but funny

jokes.text: what's weird is that a living person lived here with his parents, well into his 60s. Then they died, and he's still here, like a paralyzed Peter Pan.
jokes.setup: Jeff analyzing what's really weird about Pierce's situation
jokes.punchline: Metaphor comparing Pierce to a disabled Peter Pan
jokes.explanation: Dark but apt metaphor - captures Pierce's stunted development perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What's really weird about Pierce's situation is that he's like a disabled Peter Pan. He never grew up, he never had to deal with real consequences, and now that his body's finally catching up with his arrested development, he's shocked.

jokes.text: It's like practicing on a cadaver. Knock yourself out.
jokes.setup: Britta wanting to therapy Pierce
jokes.punchline: Jeff comparing Pierce to a corpse for practice
jokes.explanation: Dark medical school metaphor - efficiently cruel and funny

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I think Pierce could really benefit from therapy. I've been reading about transactional analysis and—

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Britta, you can't practice therapy on Pierce. That's like practicing CPR on a corpse.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Because Pierce basically is one. You'd just be going through the motions.

jokes.text: Tubular.
jokes.setup: Seeing Pierce's excessive 80s bedroom
jokes.punchline: Troy's period-appropriate one-word reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy response - economical and character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Rad.

jokes.text: I, too, lack the adjectives.
jokes.setup: Also seeing Pierce's bedroom
jokes.punchline: Abed's formal way of saying he's speechless
jokes.explanation: Abed's clinical way of expressing being overwhelmed - good contrast to Troy

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I find myself at a loss for words.

jokes.text: how does one search for a red notebook that doesn't exist without looking, touching, or creating future nightmares?
jokes.setup: Being in Pierce's disturbing bedroom
jokes.punchline: Jeff's philosophical question about their impossible situation
jokes.explanation: Good structure and captures the horror of having to search Pierce's room

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So what you're telling me is we're trapped in Pierce's bedroom, which smells like old money and regret, and the only way out is to solve a riddle that doesn't make sense?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: How is that different from any other day at Greendale?

jokes.text: Pierce keeps his dad's bowtie by his bed. Yeesh. Table for siggy Freud, party of--
jokes.setup: Finding Pierce's father's bowtie by his bed
jokes.punchline: Britta making a psychology reference about the implications
jokes.explanation: Britta's psychology reference is apt but the restaurant metaphor is awkward

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: This is a Freudian symbol of paternal authority. The fact that Pierce kept it by his bed suggests deeply repressed feelings about his father's dominance and—

jokes.text: Don't. That's not nice. - What? He was a tight-Fisted, philandering bigot who got to outlive most Bridges.
jokes.setup: Annie defending Pierce's dead father from mockery
jokes.punchline: Jeff's brutal assessment that's both specific and oddly structured
jokes.explanation: Jeff's ruthless but accurate character assessment - dark but earned

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce's father was a good man!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, his father was a racist who made his money in apartheid South Africa. He was bad. He was a bad man.

jokes.text: How about we ask rip Van racism where the codes are?
jokes.setup: Jeff suggests talking to Pierce's father's portrait
jokes.punchline: Combines Rip Van Winkle with Pierce's father's bigotry
jokes.explanation: 'Rip Van Racism' is a brilliant nickname that captures both the father's old-fashioned bigotry and death. Great wordplay.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what? Talk to the portrait. Maybe his father's wisdom will guide you.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You think that'll actually help?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Well, it's either that or we wait 20 years for you to wake up like Rip Van Winkle. Although, knowing your father, he'd probably still be angry about the same things.

jokes.text: Oh! Analyze this!
jokes.setup: Britta presumably getting startled or attacking something
jokes.punchline: Using psychology terminology as a battle cry
jokes.explanation: Britta weaponizing her psychology interest - economical and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: REPRESSED MEMORIES!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: PASSIVE AGGRESSIVE!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: COGNITIVE DISSONANCE!

jokes.text: Pierce Hawthorne's ideas for ladies? Oh, please. Blonde, long legs, with tennis racquet. Asian rockette. Big boobs, redhead, but doctor, question mark. Oh, he's not 100% on that one?
jokes.setup: Annie finding Pierce's notebook of ideal women
jokes.punchline: Reading increasingly specific and problematic entries, ending with Pierce's uncertainty about redheads
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Pierce's problematic but hilariously specific preferences

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Pierce. 'Ideal Woman #47: Woman who laughs at my jokes'? 'Ideal Woman #52: Woman who doesn't ask questions about my past'?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: 'Ideal Woman #73: Woman who is okay with me having other ideal women'?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: 'Ideal Woman #156: Woman. Any woman. Please God, any woman at this point.'

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Those are just... those are notes, Annie.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: And what is this? 'Ideal Woman #203: Should I try redheads? Unclear on redheads. Still thinking about redheads.'

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I have a process!

jokes.text: I hate reference humor.
jokes.setup: Annie experiencing scary movie tropes
jokes.punchline: Complaining about the exact thing Community does constantly
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-joke that breaks the fourth wall while staying in character - Community at its best

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Why does every horror movie have the characters split up? That's the worst idea! And why do they always go back to investigate strange noises? Just leave! And don't go in the basement, don't go in the attic, don't go anywhere alone!

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: It's like they're following some stupid formula where everyone makes the worst possible decision at every turn. Who does that in real life?

jokes.text: It's Pierce's special gym. He never let me use it, 'cause he thinks I'm a child. He's hiding the indoor swing, and I'm the child.
jokes.setup: Troy discovering Pierce's sex dungeon
jokes.punchline: Innocent interpretation of adult equipment
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocence protecting him from understanding adult content - sweet and cringe

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Oh man, what is all this stuff? Is this some kind of... exercise equipment? Pierce, you really take your fitness seriously.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, you know, gotta stay limber.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: And what's with all these chains? Are you training for some kind of escape artist act? That's actually pretty cool.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Sure, let's go with that.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: And this leather — is this like a special gym outfit? Keeps you from slipping on the equipment?

jokes.text: Forget you saw that, and then forget I knew what it was.
jokes.setup: Shirley recognizing the sex equipment
jokes.punchline: Trying to cover both Troy's and her own knowledge
jokes.explanation: Great structure showing Shirley's embarrassment at knowing about sex equipment

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that a—no, I don't know what that is. Troy, do you know what that is?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: No! I mean, I don't think so. Why would I know?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Well, I certainly don't know. We're both very innocent people.

jokes.text: pressured to what? - To fulfill her expectations. Expectations of what? Never mind. - Unsatisfied with what?
jokes.setup: Shirley hints at sexual expectations in Troy's relationship
jokes.punchline: Troy's innocent questions make the conversation increasingly uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of discomfort as Troy's innocence forces Shirley to be more explicit. Great structure.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: So Troy, how's things going with your girlfriend? You two getting pretty serious?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yeah, we've been talking about moving in together.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Moving in together? Well, you know what that means... you'll need to be ready for certain... expectations.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Expectations? Like, she wants me to do more chores?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: No, Troy. I'm talking about the intimate side of things.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Oh! You mean like, sharing a Netflix password?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: ...Sure. Yes. That.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Because I've been reading about digital security, and some experts say you shouldn't share passwords with anyone, even your—

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Troy, no.

jokes.text: Oh, well, that's hallo-Wine in cougar town for ya. Oh, ladies, I'll follow you anywhere.
jokes.setup: Abed presumably seeing something spooky
jokes.punchline: Treating real life like his TV show obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - processing reality through TV show framework

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm going to treat this like a TV show. There's always a logical explanation by the third act.

jokes.text: I remember when this show was about a community college.
jokes.setup: The episode becoming a full horror parody
jokes.punchline: Jeff breaking fourth wall to complain about genre drift
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fourth wall break that acknowledges Community's concept drift - iconic meta moment

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Hold on, hold on. Are we doing a horror episode now? Because I'm pretty sure when I signed up for this show, it was supposed to be about a community college study group, not a slasher film.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: And before anyone says anything, yes, I see the irony in me breaking the fourth wall to complain about genre drift. That's the joke.

jokes.text: cool. cool, cool, cool.
jokes.setup: Abed processing some information or situation
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase delivery
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed catchphrase - not original but iconic to the character

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm going to have to re-contextualize that entire conversation.

jokes.text: Here's to you, Cornelius. Gone, But not forgiven.
jokes.setup: Jeff toasting Pierce's dead father
jokes.punchline: Twisting the typical memorial phrase to express hatred
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark twist on memorial language - economical and perfectly Jeff

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: To Pierce's father. He's in a better place now. Specifically, heaven. Away from Pierce.

jokes.text: I totally had you guys convinced I was dead. That'll teach you to value my presence.
jokes.setup: Pierce revealing his fake death
jokes.punchline: His logic about making people appreciate him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce logic - petty and attention-seeking disguised as teaching a lesson

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I faked my own death so you'd all appreciate me more.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's insane.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Is it? When's the last time any of you thought about me fondly?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: We think about you fondly all the time.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: No you don't. You think about how annoying I am. But dead Pierce? Dead Pierce is the best Pierce.

jokes.text: 'cause I hate her.
jokes.setup: Asked why he didn't scare Vicki instead of his friends
jokes.punchline: Pierce's brutally honest reason
jokes.explanation: Pierce's child-like honesty about his motivations - economical and in character

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Because Vicki's a robot, and I didn't know if she could be scared. But I knew you guys could be. I was counting on it.

jokes.text: Ghosts can't go through doors, stupid. They're not fire.
jokes.setup: Everyone questioning Pierce's logic about hiding from ghosts
jokes.punchline: Pierce's confident but nonsensical ghost physics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic delivered with complete confidence - perfectly Pierce

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What? It's simple ghost physics. Ghosts can't see you if you can't see them. It's called the Schrödinger's ghost principle.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's not what Schrödinger's principle is.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh, so now you're a quantum physicist? I've been around ghosts before, Jeff. You close your eyes, they lose interest. It's like playing dead, but with your eyelids.

jokes.text: Uh-Uh. Levar Burton was a hard no.
jokes.setup: Pierce denies hiring anyone for his prank
jokes.punchline: Implies he tried to recruit the Star Trek actor
jokes.explanation: Absurd and specific reference that suggests Pierce's delusional ambition. Great callback and economy.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I didn't hire anybody. I tried to get William Shatner, but his agent said he was 'too busy' and 'not interested in working with me.'

jokes.text: Tell Vicki I say happy Halloween. That'll mess with her.
jokes.setup: Pierce sends a message to the party
jokes.punchline: His plan to confuse someone who hates him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce petty revenge - minimal effort but maximum perceived psychological impact.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm gonna send him a fruit basket.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That way, when he eats the fruit, he'll think of me and get confused about his hatred.

jokes.text: After a night of running in circles, trying to untie Winger knots, I'm looking for something slow.
jokes.setup: Britta explaining why she wants to watch Inspector Spacetime
jokes.punchline: Using Jeff's last name as a metaphor for complicated problems
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor turning Jeff's name into a verb for emotional complications

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I want to watch Inspector Spacetime because it's about time travel and alternate dimensions, which is basically what my life is like trying to figure out my career path.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Your life isn't complicated, Britta. It's just a Winger.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: What's that supposed to mean?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Exactly.

tmdb_overview: On the way to a Halloween party, the study group must free Pierce from his panic room and slowly comes to suspect that his mansion is haunted.
insight: Season 4's second episode coasts on character quirks despite lower-than-average joke impact ratings.
